title: Function Parameters Practice
instructions: Practice using different types of function parameters: positional, default, *args, and **kwargs.
hints: Use **kwargs to collect extra keyword arguments as a dictionary
Use *args to collect variable number of positional arguments as a tuple
Default parameters must come after required parameters

testCases.description: create_profile should build a profile dict with required fields
testCases.validation:
result = create_profile('Alice', 30)
assert result['name'] == 'Alice' and result['age'] == 30 and result['city'] == 'Unknown', 'Should create profile with name, age, and default city'

testCases.description: create_profile should include additional keyword arguments
testCases.validation:
result = create_profile('Bob', 25, city='NYC', email='bob@test.com')
assert result['city'] == 'NYC' and result['email'] == 'bob@test.com', 'Should include city override and extra kwargs'

testCases.description: sum_numbers should sum any number of arguments
testCases.validation: assert sum_numbers(1, 2, 3, 4, 5) == 15, 'Should sum all provided arguments'

testCases.description: sum_numbers should return 0 when called with no arguments
testCases.validation: assert sum_numbers() == 0, 'Should return 0 with no arguments'

testCases.description: format_message should include tags when provided
testCases.validation: assert format_message('hello', 'urgent', 'web') == '[INFO] [urgent,web] hello', 'Should format with default prefix and tags'

testCases.description: format_message should use custom prefix when specified
testCases.validation: assert format_message('error occurred', prefix='ERROR') == '[ERROR] error occurred', 'Should use custom prefix without tags'

description: Master Python function parameter types by implementing functions that use positional parameters, default values, *args for variable-length arguments, and **kwargs for keyword arguments. Learn how these parameter types work together to create flexible, reusable functions.
